name: The Wild Poppy Cafe
tagline: A cozy roadside cafe, Sebastopol style
seoDescription: The Wild Poppy is a family-run creekside cafe and market in Sebastopol, serving farm-to-table brunch and lunch with house-made focaccia, organic produce

images.heroAlt: The Wild Poppy Cafe in Sebastopol, CA

sections.heading: Creekside brunch, made from scratch
sections.subheading: Family-run West County cafe and market on Bodega Highway, serving organic, regenerative, and farm-sourced food on a patio under the oaks.
badges: Family-owned and operated by chef Martin

items.title: Family-Owned And Operated By Chef Martin
items.body: Rooted right here in Sebastopol, CA, with a real stake in doing it right.

items.title: Brunch And Lunch Built On Organic
items.body: Rooted right here in Sebastopol, CA, with a real stake in doing it right.

items.title: Everything From Scratch — House-Made Focaccia
items.body: Made with care and real ingredients — quality you can taste.

items.title: Generous Vegan And Gluten-Free Menu, With
items.body: Clear, honest pricing up front — no surprises on the final bill.

items.title: Leafy Creekside Patio Above Atascadero Creek
items.body: A standard we hold on every job, not just the easy ones.

sections.heading: A Sonoma County homecoming
body: The Wild Poppy opened in spring 2024 in the charming roadside spot just outside Sebastopol that was once home to Fork Roadhouse. Chef-owner Martin Maigaard cooked in the area at the Gypsy Cafe in the early 2010s before he and his wife Danielle moved back to Kansas. They always meant to return to Sonoma County, and when this creekside property became available, the timing was finally right.

It's a true family affair. Danielle runs front of house alongside her sister Emily, Emily's partner Andrew lends a hand in the kitchen, and the Maigaards' daughter Anya works as a food runner and baker — the chocolate chip cookies are her recipe. The kitchen leans on its own house-made tofu, scratch-baked focaccia, and farm-to-table produce, with a small market up front stocked with local wine, snacks, and gifts.
highlights: Family-owned and operated by chef Martin Maigaard and his wife Danielle
Brunch and lunch built on organic, regenerative, and biodynamic local ingredients
Everything from scratch — house-made focaccia, tofu, baked goods, and seasonal soups
Generous vegan and gluten-free menu, with a dedicated gluten-free fryer

items.title: Brunch & Breakfast
items.description: Strawberries-and-cream French toast, frittata sandwiches on house-made focaccia, and tofu rancheros — with morning mimosas if you like.

items.title: Sandwiches On House-Made Focaccia
items.description: The grilled mushroom sandwich with walnut-mushroom pâté, the house tofu banh mi, and other lunch favorites built on focaccia toasted Italian-style.

items.title: Salads & Seasonal Soups
items.description: The kale Caesar with lemon-almond vinaigrette, the Teller's salad, and a rotating soup of the day made fresh.

items.title: Tacos & Small Plates
items.description: Baja fish tacos with cornmeal-fried rock cod on organic corn tortillas, plus cosmic queso — cashew queso with pepita chimichurri — and chips.

items.title: Vegan & Gluten-Free
items.description: Clearly marked vegan options in every category, a vegan kids' menu, and a dedicated gluten-free fryer so nearly everything can be made GF.

items.title: Bakery & Coffee
items.description: Daily scones, Anya's chocolate chip cookies, and other house-baked goods alongside lattes, coffee, green juice, and local wine.

items.quote: Fresh food and amazing focaccia bread, which is toasted just like in Italy.
items.author: Verified review · Sebastopol

items.quote: Cute little cafe with several clearly labeled vegan options. Great food and atmosphere.
items.author: Verified review · Sebastopol

items.quote: The best veggie food in Sonoma County! Loved the order-at-the-counter casual feel and the amazing outdoor seating.
items.author: Verified review · Sebastopol

sections.heading: Hungry yet?
sections.body: Find us at 9890 Bodega Hwy, Sebastopol, CA 95472. Call (707) 503-6332 — we’re glad to help.
